violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment employees two Serve Safe Manager establishment must register an employee with RIDOH as a Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025
Food residue was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of sugar. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

The handwashing sink was blocked by buckets of coffee and observed to have coffee residue in it. A hand-washing sink shall only be used for handwashing and must be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

The ice scoop observed resting in ice between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
Single-Service coffee lids observed stored beside the handsink with no splashguard to protect from contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black residue and other debris. These areas must be cleaned routinely to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2024
